skill.name: docs-write
skill.description: Write and edit Metabase documentation in a clear, conversational, user-focused style for Markdown and MDX files.
skill.summary: Apply Metabase documentation style rules to drafts, edits, headings, links, examples, and formatting.
skill.author: metabase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell backticks, SAML text, and style-guide words as high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples and documentation guidance. The only confirmed risk is a legitimate formatter command using Bash, so publication is acceptable with standard command review.

low_findings.title: Benign Formatter Command Uses Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run `yarn prettier --write <file-path>` after documentation edits. This is a real external command, but it is a normal project formatter command scoped to a documentation file path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicit and uses Bash, so the external-command pattern is real. Context shows it is limited to formatting edited documentation, with no evidence of exfiltration or destructive behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Backticks Misclassified as Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged backticks inside a Markdown example that shows how to present commands in documentation. The text is instructional formatting guidance, not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside a fenced Markdown block and contain placeholder text such as command-to-run and next-command. There is no executable Ruby code or dynamic command construction.

low_findings.title: Sensitive and Reconnaissance Pattern Matches Are Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance alerts match ordinary documentation words and examples. The SAML references are identity documentation examples, and the other lines are writing-style guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found style-guide prose and SAML documentation examples, not credential theft, cryptographic code, or reconnaissance behavior. No suspicious commands, network calls, or secret access appear in these lines.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Formatter Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill may run a project formatter through Bash. This is useful for documentation consistency, but command execution should remain limited to trusted project tooling and explicit file paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The formatter command is directly present in the skill. It is not malicious in context, but it is still an external command capability.

content.value_statement: Technical documentation often becomes formal, vague, or hard to scan. This skill applies Metabase writing guidance to create clear, conversational documentation for Markdown and MDX files.
actual_capabilities: Drafts documentation with a conversational Metabase voice.
Edits headings so they state the main point clearly.
Rewrites links, UI labels, and code formatting to match style rules.
Checks documentation for vague language, outdated numbers, and weak examples.
Formats Markdown and MDX documentation after edits with project tooling.
limitations: It is tailored to Metabase documentation conventions, not every product style guide.
It does not verify product behavior unless the relevant source or docs are available.
It may run a formatter command when editing files in a compatible project.
It does not replace technical review by product or engineering owners.

use_cases.title: Create product documentation
use_cases.description: Turn feature notes into user-focused documentation with clear steps, context, and concise explanations.

use_cases.title: Edit pull request docs
use_cases.description: Review Markdown or MDX changes for tone, heading quality, link text, and formatting before merging.

use_cases.title: Standardize support content
use_cases.description: Rewrite help content so it uses familiar terms, clear actions, and Metabase terminology.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a new doc
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a first draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Metabase-style documentation draft for this feature: [feature]. Include the audience, main task, steps, and important limitations.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an existing page
prompt_templates.scenario: Editing a documentation file for clarity.
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this documentation page to match the Metabase style guide. Improve tone, headings, link text, and step order without changing the technical meaning.

prompt_templates.title: Review documentation changes
prompt_templates.scenario: Pull request review for docs quality.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these documentation changes. Flag unclear headings, vague language, outdated details, broken examples, and formatting issues.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite advanced technical guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this advanced technical guidance for Metabase readers. Preserve all constraints, explain tradeoffs clearly, and keep the action before the explanation.

output_examples.input: A draft says, "Utilize the aggregation offerings to gain actionable insights here."
output_examples.output: A clearer rewrite would use familiar words, describe the real action, and replace vague link text with a descriptive link.

output_examples.input: A page has a heading named "Environment variables".
output_examples.output: The skill would make the heading more specific, such as explaining what the variables help people configure.

output_examples.input: A step-by-step guide hides the command sequence in a long paragraph.
output_examples.output: The skill would move the commands into ordered steps, add short context, and keep the instructions in execution order.

best_practices: Provide the target audience and the exact documentation file being edited.
Share product constraints and known edge cases before asking for a rewrite.
Ask for a final review of headings, links, examples, and formatter output.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent product behavior without source material.
Do not use it for a style guide that conflicts with Metabase conventions.
Do not run formatter commands on broad paths when only one file changed.

faq.question: What kind of documentation does this skill help write?
faq.answer: It helps write and edit Metabase documentation in Markdown or MDX, including guides, explanations, and procedural content.

faq.question: Can it review existing documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review tone, structure, headings, link text, terminology, examples, and formatting.

faq.question: Does it check whether examples work?
faq.answer: It asks the assistant to verify examples when possible, but it needs project context or runnable commands to confirm behavior.

faq.question: Does it execute commands?
faq.answer: It may run the project Prettier command for formatting after edits. Review command scope before running it.

faq.question: Is this only for Metabase?
faq.answer: The guidance is written for Metabase. Other teams can adapt it, but the tone and terminology may not match their standards.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
